steps.title: Convert the radical to exponent notation
steps.content:
The seventh root becomes a fractional exponent:
$$\sqrt[7]{x^9 y^9} = (x^9 y^9)^{1/7}$$

steps.title: Factor the base using exponent rules
steps.content:
The expression $x^9 y^9$ can be written as $(xy)^9$:
$$(x^9 y^9)^{1/7} = ((xy)^9)^{1/7}$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule to simplify
steps.content:
Combine the exponents by multiplying them:
$$((xy)^9)^{1/7} = (xy)^{9 \cdot 1/7} = (xy)^{9/7}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
